narration_text: Now we'll see how packet queuing works in the context of packet-switching. Notice that when the arrival rate exceeds the transmission rate, packets will start to queue, waiting for their turn to be transmitted over the output link. Look at the diagram on your slide, you'll see a representation of how this queuing happens. The formula on the slide represents the queue of packets waiting for transmission, essentially telling us how the queue grows over time. I want you to focus on the fact that if the queue becomes too large, packets can be dropped, which is a critical issue in network design. Let's think about this together, considering the implications of packet loss on our network's performance, and we'll explore ways to mitigate this issue on the next slide.
